1.1.name: Ask and give news and update of someone
examples: Have you been in touch with her recently? How are things going with her work?
I heard she changed jobs again.
Do you have any news about him?
vocabulary: long time no see /l…íŇč ta…™m n…ô ä siňź/

1.2.name: Interact on the phone, or using chat, messaging, and email
examples: Who are you looking for? ... Please hold on.
Hello, this is ... Could you please ask Xiao Wang to come to the phone?
Sorry, I can't hear you clearly. Could you speak a little louder?
If needed, feel free to contact me anytime. By phone or email, both are fine.
Let's chat another day! Say hello to your family for me.
vocabulary: make a phone call /me…™k …ô f…ô än k…Ēňźl/

1.3.name: Express gratitude, compliments, and congratulations
examples: Thank you so much!
You've helped me so much, I don't know how to thank you!
You finally got this job. Congratulations!
A toast to our friendship!
This event was very successful, congratulations!
vocabulary: thank you so much /őł√¶Ňčk juňź s…ô ä m Ćt É/

1.4.name: Express good wishes
examples: Wishing you happiness! Good luck! Wishing you success!
Safe travels! Have a pleasant journey!
vocabulary: may all your wishes come true /me…™ …Ēňźl j…Ēňź ňąw…™ É.…™z k Ćm truňź/
have a smooth trip /h√¶v …ô smuňź√į tr…™p/

1.5.name: Express care and give encouragement
examples: You didn't pass the exam? It's okay, you'll definitely pass next time!
I feel like you've been under a lot of work pressure lately. Your health is the most important, you should take a break.
You will definitely succeed. Come on!
vocabulary: take care of yourself /te…™k ke…ôr …ôv j…Ēňźňąself/
you can do it /juňź k√¶n duňź …™t/

1.6.name: Offer or receive a gift
examples: This is a little something from me, please accept it.
This gift is too precious, you're too kind!
vocabulary: no need, no need /n…ô ä niňźd n…ô ä niňźd/

2.1.name: Ask and give specific everyday and work-related information
examples: Which department do you work in at Tsinghua University? What exactly are your responsibilities?
My current job has very few vacation days, and I often need to travel for work, so I don't have time to spend with my kids.
Has the specific itinerary for our business trip next week been arranged?
We'll fly to Beijing on Monday, adjust to the time difference on Tuesday, and meet with Director Wang on Wednesday morning...

2.2.name: Ask and give directions and instructions
examples: You need to transfer twice on the subway. After you exit the subway station, follow the signs along the road, and you'll see the person waiting for you.
For this document, review it first, make sure there are no issues, then have Director Wang sign it, and then send it to these two departments.
Take this medicine twice a day, three tablets each time, and do not take on an empty stomach.
vocabulary: how to get there /ha ä tuňź …°et √įe…ôr/

2.3.name: Ask and give a description of a person, object, or place
examples: Besides appearance, could you be more specific, for example, what is her personality like? What is her attitude toward work?
My phone is waterproof, and there's a protective film on the screen, so it hasn't broken in five years.
This meeting room is connected to the internet, has audio equipment, and a projector screen that can be raised and lowered. The rent is 1500 per month.

2.4.name: Ask and give information about an experience, situation, or event
examples: I attended this training once two years ago, my supervisor asked me to go, but there weren't as many people back then, and the activities weren't as rich.
The event started with speeches from the leaders, then remarks from the main guests, followed by some performances, and ended with the closing ceremony and dinner. Everything went very smoothly.

2.5.name: Ask and give clarification and explanation
examples: I want to know why you didn't approve my leave request.
What exactly is going on? Can you explain it more clearly?
Okay, thank you for your question, let me explain.
Why do you think you are suitable for this job?
vocabulary: what do you mean /w…ít duňź juňź miňźn/
that is to say /√į√¶t …™z tuňź se…™/

2.6.name: Understand and make an announcement
examples: Attention passengers traveling to ... Flight ... is now boarding. Please take your personal belongings, show your boarding pass, and board at gate ... Have a pleasant journey.
Attention everyone, today's document processing hours are from 8:00 AM to 12:00 PM. After 12:00, all document services will be suspended.
I have an announcement to make: ...

2.7.name: Summarize short narratives, biographies, presentations, and interviews
examples: This story is mainly about a young Iranian man's difficult immigration experience.
He was born in Guangzhou, China, with ancestral roots in Suzhou, and is a famous Chinese-American architect.
During the interview, when the reporter asked why he chose this profession, he replied...

3.1.name: Express basic feelings and emotions
examples: Really? How could they do this!
I'm very sorry, I really can't help you.
Unfortunately...
He was very nervous, of course, when he was interviewed on TV for the first time.
She was so excited she couldn't speak.

3.2.name: Express agreement and disagreement
examples: I completely agree. I support your view.
I think that's a good idea.
I don't agree with that view/opinion. I oppose it.
I don't think so. I think...

3.3.name: Express interest and indifference
examples: What you're saying is very interesting. Go on.
Sure, next time you go, remember to invite me.
Don't talk to me about this. I'm not really interested in this kind of thing.
I don't really have any opinion.

3.4.name: Express intention and willingness
examples: Who is willing to be responsible for notifying all departments? I'll do it.
I really want to work at your place for a while.

3.5.name: Express hopes, expectations, and ambitions
examples: I hope I can also work at Tsinghua University! Is it too late to apply now?
I hope this event can conclude successfully.
My goal is to be able to... within two years.

3.6.name: Express satisfaction or concerns
examples: All delegations expressed satisfaction with the meeting schedule.
The problem is...
What is worrying is...
I'm worried that if this year's budget is insufficient, this project won't be completed on time.

3.7.name: Express certainty, probability, and doubt
examples: With the joint efforts of all colleagues, this task will certainly be completed.
I'm afraid there's no time this time.
I'm wondering if he might have gotten the time wrong.
I'm not sure if he is willing to participate.

3.8.name: Express obligation and necessity
examples: I need to think about your suggestion again.
Applications must be submitted within the specified date.
I think it is necessary to bring up this issue again for discussion in this afternoon's meeting.

3.9.name: Give views and opinions
examples: How should this problem be solved? My opinion is...
I think protecting the environment is not only the responsibility of governments and international organizations, but also something every one of us should do.

3.10.name: Identify problems/issues and suggest solutions
examples: If this proposal doesn't pass, what should we do?
The main problem now is...
In the city where we live, road traffic is a big problem because the population density is high and the city streets are narrow, so there are often traffic jams.
There is only one solution to this problem: to vigorously develop green public transportation and encourage low-carbon transport.

4.1.name: Make arrangement for an appointment, order, or reservation
examples: Let's have a meeting at 10 AM on Wednesday. Do you have time? I want to discuss your work performance this year. It won't take more than an hour.
I don't eat meat. Do you have vegetarian dishes?
I want to order ten meal sets, each with an extra bottle of drink. Can you deliver them before 11:30 AM?
vocabulary: how much /ha ä m Ćt É/

4.2.name: Ask and offer help or services
examples: Can I book plane tickets here?
Is there a direct bus from here to the airport?
Do you need help?
The training department offers courses in the six UN languages.
vocabulary: let me help you /let miňź help juňź/
do you need help /duňź juňź niňźd help/

4.3.name: Authorize or forbid an act
examples: Tsinghua University's relevant departments allow and support employees working from home.
Please do not smoke indoors.
Photography is prohibited here.

4.4.name: Make and respond to a suggestion or advice
examples: Do you have any better ideas?
Try to drink less sugary drinks and eat more vegetables and fruits.
How about this: contact your colleagues in the math department to see if they can help.
I propose / I suggest...
vocabulary: not so good /n…ít s…ô ä …° äd/

4.5.name: Make and respond to a request
examples: Can you send that report by Friday?
Please let me know your decision as soon as possible.
For this document, review it first, make sure there are no issues, then have Director Wang sign it, and then send it to these two departments. Can you finish by 10 o'clock?

5.1.name: Initiate, maintain, and close conversations
examples: Shall we begin?
The topic we want to discuss today is...
Where were we?
Sorry to interrupt, let's focus on the main issue first.
We're running out of time, let's end the discussion here!

5.2.name: Request to speak and ask somebody to speak

Xiao Gao is familiar with this situation, let's have him introduce it!

what do you mean /w…ít duňź juňź miňźn/

5.3.name: Check understanding

If you have any questions, please feel free to ask.

5.4.name: Use connecting words expressing cause and effect, contrast, etc.
examples: I agree, time is tight, but from another perspective...
Therefore, it is inevitable that such a situation would occur.
